title: Colombo 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Colombo 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Colombo, Sri Lanka: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Gangaramaya Temple and National Museum of Colombo. Prices from $2049 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Colombo, Sri Lanka — Tropical climate with little seasonal temperature variation (highs 29-31°C year-round); the southwest monsoon (May-September) brings the heaviest rain to Colombo, while December-March is comparatively dry and sunny. December to March, during the drier northeast monsoon window on the west coast, with warm, humid highs around 29-31°C nearly year-round. This itinerary blends Gangaramaya Temple, National Museum of Colombo, Galle Face Green with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Gangaramaya Temple, National Museum of Colombo, Galle Face Green
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Colombo
itinerary.description: Land at Bandaranaike International Airport (CMB) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Gangaramaya Temple — Eclectic Buddhist temple complex blending multiple architectural styles. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Colombo: National Museum of Colombo & Colombo Lotus Tower
itinerary.description: Morning visit to National Museum of Colombo — Sri Lanka's largest museum, with royal regalia and ancient artifacts. In the afternoon, continue to Colombo Lotus Tower — South Asia's tallest self-supported structure, with observation decks. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Colombo: Dutch Museum, Pettah & Independence Memorial Hall
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Dutch Museum, Pettah — Colonial-era Dutch governor's residence turned museum. In the afternoon, continue to Independence Memorial Hall — National monument commemorating 1948 independence. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Negombo
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Negombo, a popular side trip from Colombo. Return to Colombo in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Seema Malaka Temple at dusk — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Colombo: Seema Malaka Temple & Galle Face Green
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Seema Malaka Temple — Meditation temple built on stilts over Beira Lake, designed by Geoffrey Bawa. In the afternoon, continue to Galle Face Green — Oceanfront promenade popular at sunset for kite-flying and street food. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Kandy
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Kandy, a popular side trip from Colombo. Return to Colombo in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Colombo: Pettah Market & Beira Lake promenade
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Pettah Market — Chaotic, colorful old-city bazaar district. In the afternoon, continue to Beira Lake promenade — Walkway around the city-center lake. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Wolvendaal Church — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Bentota beach town
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Bentota beach town, a popular side trip from Colombo. Return to Colombo in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Bentota beach town

itinerary.title: Colombo: Old Dutch Hospital Shopping Precinct & Viharamahadevi Park
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Old Dutch Hospital Shopping Precinct — Colonial-era building restored into shops and restaurants. In the afternoon, continue to Viharamahadevi Park — Colombo's largest and oldest park. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Colombo: Red Mosque (Jami Ul-Alfar) & Gangaramaya Temple
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Red Mosque (Jami Ul-Alfar) — Distinctive candy-striped mosque in Pettah. In the afternoon, continue to Gangaramaya Temple — Eclectic Buddhist temple complex blending multiple architectural styles. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Colombo Fort area colonial architecture — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to National Museum of Colombo or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to CMB for your departure flight.

travelTips: Apply for the Sri Lanka ETA online at least a few days before departure — airport-arrival processing can be slow.
Use the PickMe app for tuk-tuks and taxis to avoid price haggling with street drivers.
Galle Face Green is liveliest — and best for street food — right around sunset.
Visit Gangaramaya Temple in the late afternoon to also catch the adjoining Seema Malaka Temple at golden hour.
Pair Colombo with a short trip to Kandy or Negombo — both are under 3 hours by road or rail.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Lightweight, breathable clothing for year-round heat and humidity
Modest clothing for temple visits (shoulders/knees covered)
A light rain jacket for the May-September monsoon
Sandals suitable for barefoot temple floors
thingsToDo: Whale watching day trips from nearby Mirissa (seasonal)
Surfing lessons near Colombo's southern beaches
City cycling tours through Fort and Pettah
thingsToAvoid: Unmetered tuk-tuk drivers quoting inflated tourist fares — insist on the meter or use PickMe.
Gem "bargain" scams, particularly around Pettah and tourist hotels.
Swimming at Mount Lavinia or Galle Face beaches during rough monsoon surf — currents can be strong.
Photographing military or government installations — this is taken very seriously in Sri Lanka.
Turning your back to a Buddha statue for photos — considered a serious cultural offense.
topRestaurants: Ministry of Crab — Sri Lankan Seafood ($$$$)
Sea Spray Restaurant (Galle Face Hotel) — Seafood, oceanfront ($$$)
Nuga Gama (Cinnamon Grand) — Traditional Sri Lankan village-style ($$$)
Hoppers, Colombo — Sri Lankan street food ($)
instagramSpots: Seema Malaka Temple on Beira Lake
adventureActivities: Whale watching day trips from nearby Mirissa (seasonal)
Surfing lessons near Colombo's southern beaches
City cycling tours through Fort and Pettah
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at the historic Galle Face Hotel
Fine dining at Ministry of Crab
Spa retreat at a five-star Colombo hotel
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset cocktails at Galle Face Hotel
Private dinner at Ministry of Crab
Sunset walk along Galle Face Green
Couple's spa treatment with Ayurvedic influences
budgetExperiences: Street food (hoppers, kottu) crawl at Galle Face Green
Public bus or tuk-tuk city tour
Free entry to Independence Memorial Hall
localEtiquette: Dress modestly and remove shoes and hats when entering Buddhist and Hindu temples such as Gangaramaya.
Never turn your back to a Buddha statue for a photo — it is considered highly disrespectful.
Use your right hand for eating and giving/receiving items.
Public displays of affection are generally frowned upon.
Bargaining is common in markets like Pettah, but fixed prices are standard in malls and boutiques.
localTransportGuide: Tuk-tuks (three-wheelers) are the most common way to get around — use the PickMe app for metered, upfront pricing rather than negotiating on the street. Colombo also has a limited suburban rail network and city buses; ride-hailing apps like PickMe and Uber operate reliably in the city.
bestTimeToVisit: December to March, during the drier northeast monsoon window on the west coast, with warm, humid highs around 29-31°C nearly year-round.
weather: Tropical climate with little seasonal temperature variation (highs 29-31°C year-round); the southwest monsoon (May-September) brings the heaviest rain to Colombo, while December-March is comparatively dry and sunny.
visaInformation: Most foreign nationals, including Indian passport holders, need an Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) approved online before departure — apply via the official Sri Lanka ETA portal well in advance to avoid airport delays.
averageFlightTime: ~10.3h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Colombo?
faqs.answer: Most foreign nationals, including Indian passport holders, need an Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) approved online before departure — apply via the official Sri Lanka ETA portal well in advance to avoid airport delays.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Colombo?
faqs.answer: December to March, during the drier northeast monsoon window on the west coast, with warm, humid highs around 29-31°C nearly year-round. Tropical climate with little seasonal temperature variation (highs 29-31°C year-round); the southwest monsoon (May-September) brings the heaviest rain to Colombo, while December-March is comparatively dry and sunny.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Colombo safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Colombo is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Colombo?
faqs.answer: The local currency is LKR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Tom Tanaka
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Dutch Museum, Pettah and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Lucas Fernandez
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Red Mosque (Jami Ul-Alfar) and National Museum of Colombo. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Yuki Brown
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Viharamahadevi Park alone was worth the trip, but combined with Colombo Lotus Tower this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Anna Novak
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Colombo is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Viharamahadevi Park. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: James Cohen
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Our trip to Colombo was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Red Mosque (Jami Ul-Alfar) was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Mia van Dijk
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Old Dutch Hospital Shopping Precinct and Viharamahadevi Park. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.
